fields.title: Add 'default' mode to /mode command
fields.description:
Add a 'default' mode to the /mode slash command that restores vanilla pi behavior: no mode system prompt injection, no model override, no thinking level override.

## Design

### Type split
- `PromptModePresetName = 'smart' | 'deep' | 'rush'` — modes with presets
- `PromptModeName = 'default' | PromptModePresetName` — all modes

### Files to change

1. `src/prompt/modes.ts` — introduce `PromptModePresetName`, widen `PromptModeName` to include 'default', update `isPromptModeName`, keep `resolvePromptModePresets` returning `Record<PromptModePresetName, PromptModePreset>`
2. `src/services/prompt-modes.ts` — add 'default' to select list, `applyModeSelection` skips setModel/setThinkingLevel for 'default', `before_agent_start` returns baseSystemPrompt unchanged for 'default', `/mode list` shows 'default'
3. `src/shared/state.ts` — add 'default' to PromptModeName type and Schema.Literals
4. `src/agent/agent-registry.ts` — guard against building virtual agent for 'default'

### Behavior
- When mode is 'default': no system prompt injection, no model/thinking override
- When switching to 'default' from another mode: model stays as-is (user controls via /model)
- `resolvePersistedMode` keeps defaulting to 'smart' when no mode persisted
- 'default' is NOT a spawnable agent

### Quality gate
Run `npm run gate` from extensions/tau/ before marking done.
